full_text:
Cerebras Inference on AWS Marketplace Simplified
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Cerebras
116,125 followers
3mo
Edited
Report this post
💡 Partner Spotlight: AWS Marketplace
Cerebras brings the fastest inference. Amazon Web Services (AWS) Marketplace brings the simplest way to buy and deploy it.
Together, we enable teams to start building faster inside the AWS environment they already use.
What this unlocks:
🟧 Access Cerebras Inference Cloud through AWS → deploy faster inside your existing environment
🟧 Consolidated AI spend → roll inference into your current AWS billing
🟧 Use EDP and committed spend → maximize existing cloud investments
🟧 Simplified procurement → streamline purchasing, billing, and internal approvals
🟧 Monthly marketplace billing → track and manage AI costs alongside AWS services
Download the ebook: Achieve Fast, Accurate AI with AWS + Cerebras https://lnkd.in/e9ZEmjwd
👉 Get started: https://lnkd.in/gAveM6yi

You can now build and test cloud apps completely offline without an internet connection.. 🤯
Someone built gofakes3, a lightweight S3 clone for testing without AWS. It lets you mock cloud storage directly on your machine.

- Lightweight and easy to set up.
100% open source.

Last month at re:Invent, we launched AWS Professional Services offerings on AWS Marketplace – a significant advancement in how customers access specialized expertise to accelerate their cloud transformation.
What We Launched: Nine specialized offerings across three strategic themes:
• Data & AI
• Migration and Modernization
• Contact Center Solutions
The Impact: This launch streamlines how customers discover and engage with AWS ProServe Solutions through Marketplace, creating a more efficient path from identifying needs to implementation.
Key Customer Benefits:
✓ Simplified discovery and procurement process
✓ Direct access to AWS expertise and proven solutions
✓ Accelerated time-to-value for cloud initiatives
📋 Explore the offerings: https://go.aws/4rIxtPz
🔗 Learn more: https://go.aws/412SCIZ

I like how Amazon Web Services (AWS) uses their login page to tell you about their other products and services available.
Like here I'm finding out about AWS Elemental Inference.
A service that runs AI inference directly on video streams, to convert broadcasts to vertical video in real time without sending all video back to the cloud.

Compared to an empty login page, this space becomes an opportunity to communicate value.
It's a guaranteed touchpoint.
Users see it every time they access the system.
A short highlight about a feature, service, or capability captures attention without requiring extra effort from the user.
Users discover tools or capabilities they didn’t know existed, potentially solving problems faster or unlocking new workflows, turning a normally idle page into a moment of value.

Heru Abdillah Sajad
AWS Explorer
2mo
Report this post
💰 Continuing my AWS journey — today's topic: cost management.
AWS has 4 built-in tools to monitor, analyze, and optimize your cloud spending.
Knowing which tool to use — and when — can save you from a very unpleasant billing surprise.
1. Cost Explorer
Visualize and analyze your historical usage and spending patterns across services, accounts, and time periods.
2. AWS Budgets
Set custom cost thresholds and receive alerts before your spending exceeds a defined limit.
3. AWS Compute Optimizer
Get machine learning–based recommendations for right-sizing your resources — so you're not paying for capacity you don't need.
4. AWS Pricing Calculator
Estimate the cost of your architecture before you deploy — great for planning and budgeting new workloads.
Cloud cost management isn't just about cutting costs — it's about spending wisely and knowing exactly where every dollar goes.
#AWS #CloudComputing #FinOps #LearningInPublic #TechJourney

InterWorks
24,507 followers
2mo
Report this post
Building an AI agent in AWS is more approachable than you might think.
In his latest blog, Leopold Ringmayr walks through Amazon Bedrock from the ground up, using a coffee shop customer service agent as a hands-on example.
From selecting a foundation model to configuring guardrails, connecting a knowledge base and wiring up a Lambda function to actually place orders, each piece comes together in a single managed platform.
It's a practical look at one of three paths enterprises typically face when deploying AI:
🔹 Go direct through a model provider
🔹 Run models locally on your own infrastructure
🔹 Use a managed cloud service
Bedrock sits in that middle ground, and Leopold breaks down when that tradeoff makes sense. Read the full walkthrough at the link below.
🏗️ https://lnkd.in/gMiMxWAA

Alejandra Yonet Garza Delanoé
3mo
Report this post
I started exploring Amazon Sagemaker while learning more about ML workflows in the cloud.
One thing I find Interesting it how it centralized different parts of ML lifecycle, from dataset preparation and training jobs to experimenting tracking an model deployment.
Still exploring the ecosystem, but it’s been interesting to see how cloud platform are shaping the way modern ML systems are built.
Courious to hear how others are using Sagemaker or similar tools in their workflows.
#MachineLearning #AWS

CLOUD CHARIOTS
668 followers
2mo
Report this post
Many businesses are sitting on valuable data — but very few are actually using it to make smarter decisions.
With services like Amazon Redshift, Amazon QuickSight, Amazon Athena and Amazon Bedrock, organizations can turn raw data into dashboards, insights, interactive analysis and AI-powered capabilities that improve how they operate.
But the real challenge isn’t the technology — it’s knowing how to implement it properly for your business.
At Cloud Chariots, we help businesses:
1. Design the right AWS architecture for their needs
2. Deploy scalable analytics and AI solutions
3. Optimize costs and performance from day one.
To make adoption easier, we also help eligible businesses access AWS credits to kickstart their cloud journey.
Because the right cloud strategy doesn’t just store data — it creates business advantages.

The eBook, "Getting Started with Generative AI on AWS," outlines why many organizations build on AWS, from enterprise-grade security and privacy to low-cost, high-performance infrastructure and data-driven customization.
Download the eBook to see how AWS supports responsible, scalable generative AI at every layer of the stack. @Amazon Web Services (AWS) #GenerativeAI #AWSCloud

💰 Continuing my AWS journey — today's topic: cost management.
AWS has 4 built-in tools to monitor, analyze, and optimize your cloud spending.
Knowing which tool to use — and when — can save you from a very unpleasant billing surprise.
1. Cost Explorer
Visualize and analyze your historical usage and spending patterns across services, accounts, and time periods.
2. AWS Budgets
Set custom cost thresholds and receive alerts before your spending exceeds a defined limit.
3. AWS Compute Optimizer
Get machine learning–based recommendations for right-sizing your resources — so you're not paying for capacity you don't need.
4. AWS Pricing Calculator
Estimate the cost of your architecture before you deploy — great for planning and budgeting new workloads.
Cloud cost management isn't just about cutting costs — it's about spending wisely and knowing exactly where every dollar goes.
#AWS #CloudComputing #FinOps #LearningInPublic #TechJourney

Building an AI agent in AWS is more approachable than you might think.
In his latest blog, Leopold Ringmayr walks through Amazon Bedrock from the ground up, using a coffee shop customer service agent as a hands-on example.
From selecting a foundation model to configuring guardrails, connecting a knowledge base and wiring up a Lambda function to actually place orders, each piece comes together in a single managed platform.
It's a practical look at one of three paths enterprises typically face when deploying AI:
🔹 Go direct through a model provider
🔹 Run models locally on your own infrastructure
🔹 Use a managed cloud service
Bedrock sits in that middle ground, and Leopold breaks down when that tradeoff makes sense. Read the full walkthrough at the link below.
